title: Semiya Upma

ingredients.name: wheat vermicelli (semiya)
visual_checks: roasted or plain; plain needs 2 minutes of dry roasting first
ingredients.notes: roasted or plain; plain needs 2 minutes of dry roasting first

subs.name: rice vermicelli (rice noodles)
subs.notes: makes it gluten-free; do not roast rice noodles; cook them in boiling water for 2–3 minutes and drain before adding to tempering

subs.name: 3 shallots, finely sliced
subs.notes: sweeter flavour; small onions are preferred in Chettinad homes

subs.name: moong-dal
subs.notes: softer texture; soak 10 minutes before frying if substituting whole moong

steps.text: If using plain (un-roasted) vermicelli, dry-roast it in a kadai on medium flame, stirring constantly, until pale golden and fragrant — about 2 minutes. Set aside. If using pre-roasted vermicelli, skip this step.
Heat oil in the same kadai. Splutter mustard seeds, then add urad dal and chana dal and fry until golden. Add curry leaves, green chilli and ginger and saute for 30 seconds.
Add the onion with a pinch of salt and saute on medium flame until soft, about 3 minutes.
Pour in 2.5 cups water, add salt and bring to a boil on high flame.

Add the roasted vermicelli to the boiling water, stirring immediately. Reduce to medium flame, cover and cook for 3–4 minutes until all the water is absorbed and the strands are just tender but not mushy. Taste one strand — it should be cooked through but still have a slight bite.
Uncover and fluff gently with a fork. Scatter coriander leaves on top. Serve hot with coconut chutney. Semiya upma is best eaten right away; it clumps as it cools.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: vermicelli → rice vermicelli (rice noodles) (makes it gluten-free; do not roast rice noodles; cook them in boiling water for 2–3 minutes and drain before adding to tempering); onion → 3 shallots, finely sliced (sweeter flavour; small onions are preferred in Chettinad homes); chana-dal → moong-dal (softer texture; soak 10 minutes before frying if substituting whole moong)
